predicate.title: Metrics integrity audit: token usage, lead time, cycle time
predicate.problem: Owner audit of the delivered metrics/dashboard slice (roadmap-priority-1-metrics) found lead time, cycle time, and token usage all read as wrong on the dashboard even though the underlying per-row arithmetic is individually correct. Three distinct defects: (1) bindings/claude-code/usage.py's newest_transcript() hardcodes a stale session-transcript directory, so token-usage evidence collection silently fails for every worktree-per-change session -- 10 of 12 Change Records have no usage.json; (2) where token evidence does exist, cache_read_input_tokens is summed across the whole window and scales with turns x accumulated context rather than work actually done, so it is not a work-scoped figure; (3) the lead/cycle time SVG chart in render_html() derives one shared linear axis from every merged row's raw value, so acknowledged bootstrap negative-time rows and one genuine overnight-gap outlier stretch the axis until the other 9 of 12 normal-magnitude rows render as near-invisible slivers.
acceptance_criteria: A new roadmap-item (roadmap-priority-2-metrics-integrity) documents all three findings and is wired into the roadmap doc-manifest ahead of every not-yet-started slice
docs/design/roadmap.md is regenerated via scaffold.py with no validation errors
The roadmap-item links back to roadmap-priority-1-metrics and to the metric-token-usage, metric-lead-time, and metric-cycle-time nodes it corrects
